signals.headline: Goldman Sachs released its U
signals.raw:
Goldman Sachs released its U.S. Inflation Monitor on April 20, 2026**, revising its December 2026 headline PCE inflation forecast **upward by a full percentage point**, with most of the revision attributable to energy costs[1]. The bank estimates the Iran conflict will add roughly 0.1 percentage points to headline inflation through higher fertilizer and food prices[1].

Consumer inflation expectat
matched_markets: Will annual inflation increase by 3.8% in April?

signals.headline: The most significant confirmed development is that **headline CPI surged 0
signals.raw: The most significant confirmed development is that **headline CPI surged 0.87% in March alone, reaching 3.29% year-over-year**, driven primarily by a 45% spike in oil prices tied to the start of the Iran war[1]. Oil prices jumped from $71 per barrel in February to $103 in March[1], creating an immediate shock to inflation expectations across markets.
